- llm-council: Run any question, idea, or decision through a council of 5 AI advisors who independently analyze it, peer-review each other anonymously, and synthesize a final verdict. Based on Karpathy's LLM Council methodology. MANDATORY TRIGGERS: 'council this', 'run the council', 'war room this', 'pressure-test this', 'stress-test this', 'debate this'. STRONG TRIGGERS (use when combined with a real decision or tradeoff): 'should I X or Y', 'which option', 'what would you do', 'is this the right move', 'validate this', 'get multiple perspectives', 'I can't decide', 'I'm torn between'. Do NOT trigger on simple yes/no questions, factual lookups, or casual 'should I' without a meaningful tradeoff (e.g. 'should I use markdown' is not a council question). DO trigger when the user presents a genuine decision with stakes, multiple options, and context that suggests they want it pressure-tested from multiple angles.

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. ## 3. Core business logic

There are **two** relevant files here — the orchestrator in `src/lib/core/` and the actual time-check logic, which lives in `src/lib/invitations.ts` (not under `src/lib/core/`).

**`/Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/lib/core/triggerInvitations/triggerInvitations.ts`** (orchestrator — calls the decision logic then conditionally sends)

...

29	    data: NewGameInput,
30	    deps: TriggerInvitationsDeps = defaultDeps,
31	) {
32	    const customMessage = data.customMessage?.trim();
33	    const decision = await deps.getInvitationDecision(data.overrideTimeCheck);
34	
35	    if (decision.status === 'ready' && decision.gameDayId) {
36	        await deps.sendGameInvitations(decision.gameDayId, customMessage);
37	    }
38

**`/Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/lib/invitations.ts`** — this is the actual override/9am-day-before check:
```ts
89	export async function getInvitationDecision(override = false): Promise<InvitationDecision> {
90	    const upcomingGame = await gameDayService.getUpcoming();
91	
92	    if (!upcomingGame) {
93	        return {
94	            status: 'skipped',
95	            reason: 'no-upcoming-game',
96	        };
97	    }
98	
99	    if (upcomingGame.mailSent && !override) {
100	        return {
101	            status: 'skipped',
102	            reason: 'already-sent',
103	            gameDayId: upcomingGame.id,
104	            gameDate: upcomingGame.date,
105	        };
106	    }
107	
108	    // Skip the external bank-holidays fetch when the caller has already said
109	    // "send regardless of timing" — the mail date is irrelevant in that case.
110	    if (override) {
111	        return {
112	            status: 'ready',
113	            reason: 'ready',
114	            gameDayId: upcomingGame.id,
115	            gameDate: upcomingGame.date,
116	        };
117	    }
118	
119	    const mailDate = await getMailDate(upcomingGame.date);
120	    const shouldSend = new Date() >= mailDate;
121	
122	    return {
123	        status: shouldSend ? 'ready' : 'skipped',
124	        reason: shouldSend ? 'ready' : 'too-early',
125	        gameDayId: upcomingGame.id,
126	        gameDate: upcomingGame.date,
127	        mailDate,
128	    };
129	}
```

`getMailDate` (lines 44-55) computes 9:00am on the last working day before the game, walking backwards over weekends/bank holidays via `isWorkingDay` from `@/lib/bankHolidays`.

## Where the override actually gets short-circuited

`getInvitationDecision` is called as `deps.getInvitationDecision(data.overrideTimeCheck)` — a plain boolean positional arg, matching `override = false` param. Given that, ticking the checkbox does correctly bypass both the `mailSent` check (line 99, `!override`) and the mail-date/time check (line 110-117) — this is covered by an explicit unit test (`invitations.vitest.spec.ts`, "returns ready when override is true even if mail was sent").

The one check that **is never bypassed by override** is line 92:
```ts
92	    if (!upcomingGame) {
93	        return { status: 'skipped', reason: 'no-upcoming-game' };
94	    }
```
`upcomingGame` comes from `gameDayService.getUpcoming()`:

**`/Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/services/GameDay/GameDay.ts`**
```ts
132	    async getUpcoming(from: Date = new Date()): Promise<GameDayType | null> {
133	        const where = GameDayWhereInputObjectSchema.parse({
134	            game: true,
135	            date: { gte: from },
136	        });
137	        return prisma.gameDay.findFirst({ where, orderBy: { date: 'asc' } });
138	    }
```
This requires a `GameDay` row with `game: true` and `date >= now`. If there is no such row (e.g. the next game day hasn't been created yet, or the flag `game` is false for it), `getInvitationDecision` returns `{ status: 'skipped', reason: 'no-upcoming-game' }` **regardless of the override checkbox**, and `triggerInvitationsCore` (line 35) never calls `sendGameInvitations`. In the UI this surfaces as the "Invitations skipped" / "Skipped: no upcoming game" notification rather than an error — easy to read as "silently skipped" since ticking the box visibly has no effect.

The other place to double check is `src/lib/core/triggerInvitations/triggerInvitations.ts` line 35: `if (decision.status === 'ready' && decision.gameDayId)` — this only sends when `gameDayId` is truthy, so a falsy id (0/undefined) would also silently skip sending even with `status: 'ready'`, though in practice DB ids are non-zero so this is a lower-probability cause.

Everything else in the override path (`NewGameInputSchema`, `NewGameForm`'s checkbox binding via `form.getInputProps('overrideTimeCheck', { type: 'checkbox' })`, the server action, and `triggerInvitationsCore`) passes the boolean through unchanged and is covered by passing unit tests, so the most likely root cause of "checking override doesn't send" is that `gameDayService.getUpcoming()` returns `null` for the current game day (no `GameDay` row with `game: true` and a future `date`), which short-circuits before the override logic is ever consulted.</result>

89	export async function getInvitationDecision(override = false): Promise<InvitationDecision> {
90	    const upcomingGame = await gameDayService.getUpcoming();
91	
92	    if (!upcomingGame) {
93	        return {
94	            status: 'skipped',
95	            reason: 'no-upcoming-game',
96	        };
97	    }
98

102	            reason: 'already-sent',
103	            gameDayId: upcomingGame.id,
104	            gameDate: upcomingGame.date,
105	        };
106	    }
107	
108	    // Skip the external bank-holidays fetch when the caller has already said
109	    // "send regardless of timing" — the mail date is irrelevant in that case.
110	    if (override) {
111	        return {
112	            status: 'ready',
113	            reason: 'ready',
114	            gameDayId: upcomingGame.id,
115	            gameDate: upcomingGame.date,
116	        };
117	    }
118

120	    const shouldSend = new Date() >= mailDate;
121

124	        reason: shouldSend ? 'ready' : 'too-early',
125	        gameDayId: upcomingGame.id,
126	        gameDate: upcomingGame.date,
127	        mailDate,
128	    };
129	}

`getMailDate` (lines 44-55) computes 9:00am on the last working day before the game, walking backwards over weekends/bank holidays via `isWorkingDay` from `@/lib/bankHolidays`.

## Where the override actually gets short-circuited

`getInvitationDecision` is called as `deps.getInvitationDecision(data.overrideTimeCheck)` — a plain boolean positional arg, matching `override = false` param. Given that, ticking the checkbox does correctly bypass both the `mailSent` check (line 99, `!override`) and the mail-date/time check (line 110-117) — this is covered by an explicit unit test (`invitations.vitest.spec.ts`, "returns ready when override is true even if mail was sent").

The one check that **is never bypassed by override** is line 92:
```ts
92	    if (!upcomingGame) {
93	        return { status: 'skipped', reason: 'no-upcoming-game' };
94	    }
```
`upcomingGame` comes from `gameDayService.getUpcoming()`:

**`/Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/services/GameDay/GameDay.ts`**
```ts
132	    async getUpcoming(from: Date = new Date()): Promise<GameDayType | null> {
133	        const where = GameDayWhereInputObjectSchema.parse({
134	            game: true,
135	            date: { gte: from },
136	        });
137	        return prisma.gameDay.findFirst({ where, orderBy: { date: 'asc' } });
138	    }
```
This requires a `GameDay` row with `game: true` and `date >= now`. If there is no such row (e.g. the next game day hasn't been created yet, or the flag `game` is false for it), `getInvitationDecision` returns `{ status: 'skipped', reason: 'no-upcoming-game' }` **regardless of the override checkbox**, and `triggerInvitationsCore` (line 35) never calls `sendGameInvitations`. In the UI this surfaces as the "Invitations skipped" / "Skipped: no upcoming game" notification rather than an error — easy to read as "silently skipped" since ticking the box visibly has no effect.

The other place to double check is `src/lib/core/triggerInvitations/triggerInvitations.ts` line 35: `if (decision.status === 'ready' && decision.gameDayId)` — this only sends when `gameDayId` is truthy, so a falsy id (0/undefined) would also silently skip sending even with `status: 'ready'`, though in practice DB ids are non-zero so this is a lower-probability cause.

Everything else in the override path (`NewGameInputSchema`, `NewGameForm`'s checkbox binding via `form.getInputProps('overrideTimeCheck', { type: 'checkbox' })`, the server action, and `triggerInvitationsCore`) passes the boolean through unchanged and is covered by passing unit tests, so the most likely root cause of "checking override doesn't send" is that `gameDayService.getUpcoming()` returns `null` for the current game day (no `GameDay` row with `game: true` and a future `date`), which short-circuits before the override logic is ever consulted.</result>

content.text:
Typecheck, lint, and knip all pass clean, and the e2e test now passes reliably.

**Root cause:** `NewGameForm`'s "Send invitations" button had no hydration guard, so a Playwright click could land before React attached the submit handler — the click was a no-op (nothing thrown, no navigation, form values untouched), which is exactly what the stale report showed: checkbox still ticked, message still filled, but no notification ever appeared. This is the identical bug that commit `7e947051` ("Set loading state for submit button in New Game form until mounted") already fixed for `GameInvitationResponseForm`'s "Save Response" button — that commit's own message noted it "probably should be extended to all forms," but `NewGameForm` was never updated.

**Fix applied** (mirroring the existing pattern):
- `src/components/NewGameForm/NewGameForm.tsx`: added `useMounted()` from `@mantine/hooks` and `loading={!mounted}` on the submit button.
- `e2e/newgame.spec.ts`: wait for the button to be enabled before clicking, same as the `Save Response` button's test.

Verified by rebuilding and running `e2e/newgame.spec.ts` against chromium — all 3 tests pass.

file.content:
name: Playwright Tests
on:
    push:
        branches: [main]
    pull_request:
        branches: [main]
    workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
    test:
        timeout-minutes: 30
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest
        permissions:
            contents: read
            packages: read

        strategy:
            fail-fast: false
            matrix:
                include:
                    - project: chromium
                      browser: chromium
                    - project: firefox
                      browser: firefox
                    - project: webkit
                      browser: webkit
                    - project: mobile
                      browser: chromium
                    - project: tablet
                      browser: chromium

        env:
            # Hardcoded CI-only values — none of these are production secrets.
            DATABASE_PASSWORD: ci_test_password
            DATABASE_URL: REDACTED

            # Any non-empty string works for Better Auth in tests (used for
            # signing tokens; tests bypass auth via mock cookies).
            BETTER_AUTH_SECRET: ci-better-auth-secret-for-playwright-tests-only

            # Dummy OAuth credentials — tests use cookie-based mock auth and
            # never trigger a real OAuth flow.
            AUTH_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID: dummy-google-client-id
            AUTH_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET: dummy-google-client-secret
            AUTH_MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID: dummy-microsoft-client-id
            AUTH_MICROSOFT_CLIENT_SECRET: dummy-microsoft-client-secret

            # Sentry source-map upload is disabled when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN is
            # absent (see next.config.mjs disableSourceMapUpload). No token
            # needed here.

            # Pulled by docker-compose.ci.yml in place of a bare mariadb:lts,
            # pre-loaded with schema + seed data by
            # publish-seeded-db-image.yml. `prisma db push` below still runs
            # regardless, as a cheap safety net for branches whose schema
            # changes haven't been baked into this image yet.
            SEEDED_DB_IMAGE: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/next-www-toastboy-db-test-seeded:latest

        steps:
            - uses: actions/checkout@v7
            - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
              with:
                  node-version: 24
            - name: Install dependencies
              run: npm ci
            - name: Log in to GHCR
              uses: docker/login-action@v4
              with:
                  registry: ghcr.io
                  username: ${{ github.actor }}
                  password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
            - name: Start docker stack (database, mailpit, etc.)
              run: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.ci.yml up -d
            - name: Wait for database
              run: |
                  for i in {1..30}; do
                    if [ "$(docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}' $(docker compose ps -q db))" = "healthy" ]; then
                      exit 0
                    fi
                    sleep 2
                  done
                  docker compose logs db
                  exit 1
            - name: Push Prisma schema
              run: npx prisma db push
            - name: Build Next.js app
              run: npm run build
            - name: Install Playwright Browser (${{ matrix.browser }})
              run: npx playwright install --with-deps ${{ matrix.browser }}
            - name: Run Playwright tests (${{ matrix.project }})
              env:
                  # Enables cookie-based mock auth in the production-built server
                  # (see src/lib/auth.server.ts isMockAuthEnabled).
                  PLAYWRIGHT_TEST: "true"
                  # The db container above already has seed data baked in.
                  SKIP_DB_SEED: "true"
              run: npx playwright test --project=${{ matrix.project }}
            - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
              if: always()
              with:
                  name: playwright-report-${{ matrix.project }}
                  path: playwright-report/
                  retention-days: 30

file.content:
name: Publish Seeded Test Database Image
on:
    push:
        branches: [main]
        paths:
            - prisma/schema.prisma
            - prisma/seed.ci.ts
            - .github/workflows/publish-seeded-db-image.yml
    workflow_dispatch:

env:
    IMAGE: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}/next-www-toastboy-db-test-seeded

jobs:
    publish:
        timeout-minutes: 20
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest
        permissions:
            contents: read
            packages: write

        env:
            DATABASE_PASSWORD: ci_test_password
            DATABASE_URL: REDACTED

        steps:
            - uses: actions/checkout@v7
            - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
              with:
                  node-version: 24
            - name: Install dependencies
              run: npm ci

            # seed.ci.ts imports the generated client from prisma/generated/client.
            # `npm ci` doesn't produce it (no postinstall hook) - only `npm run
            # build`/`dev` do, via their own explicit `npm run generate` prefix -
            # so it must be generated explicitly here too, before anything tries
            # to import it.
            - name: Generate Prisma client
              run: npm run generate

            # A bare `docker run`, not `docker compose up`: docker-compose.yml's
            # healthcheck isn't available outside compose, so readiness is
            # polled directly below instead.
            - name: Start a bare MariaDB container to seed
              run: |
                  docker run -d --name seed-db \
                    -e MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD="REDACTED" \
                    -p 33306:3306 \
                    mariadb:lts
            # MariaDB's official image initialises the data directory with a
            # *temporary* server started with --skip-networking (socket only),
            # then shuts it down and starts the real network-facing server.
            # Probing over the unix socket (-h localhost) gives a false positive
            # against that temporary server, so the next step connects over TCP
            # mid-restart and gets "P1017: Server has closed the connection".
            # Force the probe over TCP (which the temporary server can't answer)
            # and require several consecutive successes so we don't catch the
            # brief window while the temporary server is swapped for the real one
            # - this matches how Prisma connects.
            - name: Wait for database
              run: |
                  ok=0
                  for i in {1..60}; do
                    if docker exec seed-db mariadb-admin ping --protocol=tcp -h 127.0.0.1 -u root -p"$DATABASE_PASSWORD" 2>/dev/null; then
                      ok=$((ok + 1))
                      if [ "$ok" -ge 3 ]; then
                        exit 0
                      fi
                    else
                      ok=0
                    fi
                    sleep 2
                  done
                  docker logs seed-db
                  exit 1
            - name: Apply schema
              run: npx prisma db push
            - name: Seed database
              run: npm run seed:ci

            # Stop (not kill) so InnoDB flushes cleanly before the container's
            # filesystem state is captured - an unclean shutdown risks the next
            # container needing crash recovery on first boot.
            - name: Stop database cleanly
              run: docker stop seed-db

            # Copied out and rebuilt via Dockerfile.seeded-db rather than
            # `docker commit`: commit bakes the source container's full
            # runtime config - including the -e MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD set
            # above - into the new image's metadata, readable by anyone who
            # later pulls it. A plain COPY only bakes in what the Dockerfile
            # declares, so the password never reaches the published image.
            - name: Copy seeded data directory out of the container
              run: docker cp seed-db:/var/lib/mysql ./seeded-datadir

            - name: Log in to GHCR
              uses: docker/login-action@v4
              with:
                  registry: ghcr.io
                  username: ${{ github.actor }}
                  password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
            - name: Build and push image
              run: |
                  docker build -f Dockerfile.seeded-db -t "$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }}" -t "$IMAGE:latest" .
                  docker push "$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }}"
                  docker push "$IMAGE:latest"

lines: * This function only inserts/replaces data, which is safe while the server
- * When SKIP_DB_SEED is set, seeding is skipped entirely - used by CI when the
+ * When SKIP_DB_SEED is set, the full seed is skipped - used by CI when the
* `db` container was started from a pre-seeded image (see
* `mariadb:lts`, so the data is already there. `prisma db push` still runs as
* a separate CI step regardless, so schema changes on a branch not yet
* baked into that image are still applied correctly.
+ * That image is only rebuilt when prisma/schema.prisma or prisma/seed.ci.ts
+ * change, so the single "upcoming" game day it bakes in goes stale as real
+ * time passes it - once its date is in the past, gameDayService.getUpcoming()
+ * finds no game at all and invitation-sending is skipped regardless of the
+ * override flag. So even when the full seed is skipped, that one row's date
+ * is still re-pointed at the next Friday from now.
-        console.log('⚙️  Skipping database seed - using pre-seeded image');
+        console.log('⚙️  Refreshing upcoming game day on pre-seeded image...');

content.text:
All green. Here's the actual root cause and fix.

**Real root cause:** In CI, `playwright.yml` sets `SKIP_DB_SEED: true` and pulls a pre-built database image (`next-www-toastboy-db-test-seeded`) instead of reseeding, to save time. That image is only rebuilt when `prisma/schema.prisma` or `prisma/seed.ci.ts` change — it was last rebuilt 2026-07-02 (commit `4e46b8b9`), 15 days before today. The seed bakes in a single "upcoming" game day dated `nextFriday(now)` **at build time**. By the time CI runs today, that date (2026-07-03) is 15 days in the past, so `gameDayService.getUpcoming()` (`date: { gte: now }`) finds no game at all — `getInvitationDecision` returns `no-upcoming-game` before the override flag is ever consulted. That's why ticking the override checkbox had no effect: the check it bypasses was never reached.

(My earlier fix — a `useMounted()` hydration guard on the submit button, mirroring an existing pattern from `GameInvitationResponseForm` — was a real but separate improvement; it wasn't the actual cause of this failure, which is why it didn't fix it.)

**Fix:**
- `prisma/seedDates.ts` — extracted the `nextFriday()` helper (now shared).
- `prisma/refresh-upcoming-gameday.ts` — new script that finds the game day with `game: true, mailSent: null` (the seed's unique marker for the placeholder, robust even if its date has drifted behind real historical games) and re-dates it to the next Friday from *now*.
